A.error_quality: structured - typed named diagnostics with line/caret/hints; 'Expected { after variant name (got LPAREN)' revealed that union variants carry payload, unlocking the Result-like error channel.
A.notes: Pattern A (spec + testcases + rubric only; NO grammar guide, NO other solutions read). Error channel = payload-carrying union 'union OpResult { Ok { balance: int } Err { code: int } }'; deposit/withdraw return OpResult and the Err variant is the rejection signal (callers branch via is_err()/match, not a print). Harness for #7-#12 asserts BOTH is_err==1 AND balance unchanged. Invariant balance>=0 enforced by hand-written guards + the type-distinct error channel (not a language-verified contract). Syntax (incl. the payload-union discovery) recovered purely from interpreter diagnostics on throwaway probes. Independently verified by the report author: source inspected + container re-run twice, deterministic 'TOTAL_PASSED 14', exit 0.

Expected '(' after variant name in match -- match binds variant payload with (binding)
Cannot determine struct type for field access -- nested struct payload not resolvable -> primitive int payloads

B.error_quality: free - deposit/withdraw return Result<Account,string> (Ok{value}/Err{error}); rejection carries a descriptive message; caller pattern-matches.
B.notes: Invariant enforcement = AI hand-written checks + immutable structure. Account is an immutable struct {balance:int}; deposit/withdraw are pure functions returning Result: Ok carries a NEW Account, Err keeps the OLD one, so error cannot corrupt state. withdraw rejects amount>balance before constructing the new account; both reject amount<=0, so a new Account is only built with balance>=0. Statically typed and shadow tests ran, but the >=0 invariant is enforced by explicit guards, not a refinement type.
